content.text:
Advanced Networking
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UET1  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
This module aims to consolidate and deepen students' knowledge of computer networks, addressing advanced topics such as dynamic routing, transmission protocols, application protocols and IPv6 configuration.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Computer networks (bachelor's degree).
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Network Review
OSI and TCP/IP models
MAC/IP addressing (ARP Protocol)
Subnet addressing (FLSM, VLSM, CIDR…)
Routing Protocols
Static routing
Dynamic routing (RIP, OSPF, BGP): distance vectors; link state
Local and Wide Area Networks
Virtual LANs (VLANs), VTP and DTP protocols
Inter-VLAN routing
LAN redundancy (STP, EtherChannel, HSRP)
PPP protocol
VPN (GRE protocol)
Transmission Protocols
TCP/UDP protocols
TCP connection state management
Flow control, congestion control, error control
Application Protocols
Web protocols (HTTP and HTTPS)
Email protocols (SMTP, POP and IMAP)
File sharing services (FTP and SMB)
DHCP, DNS, Telnet and SSH
IPv6 Protocol
Addressing
IPv4/IPv6 transition
IPv6 services
Network Management
LLDP, CDP, NTP
SNMP
Quality of Service (QoS)
Configuration management automation (Ansible…)
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Tanenbaum, A., Feamster, N., & Witherall, D. (2021).
Computer networks
(6th ed.). Pearson Publisher.
White, R., & Banks, E. (2017).
Computer networking problems and solutions
. Addison-Wesley Professional.
Englander, I., & Wong, W. (2021).
The architecture of computer hardware, systems software, and networking
. John Wiley & Sons.
Bonaventure, O. (2021).
Computer networking: Principles, protocols and practice
.
Dordal, P. L. (2022).
An introduction to computer networks
. Loyola University Chicago.
